events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON. Let's analyze schedule. Need determine project finish delay workdays due to J Insulation needing 2 extra workdays. Need identify critical path and float.

Image: lookahead schedule early-start bars, durations. Activities:
A Mobilize & layout [2d] predecessor -, starts 0 ends 2
B Underground rough-in [3d] <- A, starts 2 ends 5
C Slab pour [2d] <- B, starts 5 ends 7
CC Slab cure [3d] <- C, starts 7 ends 10 (hatched lag)
D Framing [5d] <- CC, starts 10 ends 15
E Roof dry-in [2d] <- D, starts 15 ends 17
F Electrical rough [3d] <- E, starts 17 ends 20
G Plumbing top-out [2d] <- E, starts 17 ends 19
H HVAC rough [3d] <- E, starts 17 ends 20
I Rough inspection [1d] <- F,G,H, starts 20 ends 21 (red)
J Insulation [2d] <- I, starts 21 ends 23
K Insulation inspection [1d] <- J, starts 23 ends 24 (red)
L Drywall hang [3d] <- K, starts 24 ends 27
M Tape & finish [6d] <- L, starts 27 ends 33
N Paint [3d] <- M, starts 33 ends 36
O Trim carpentry [3d] <- N, starts 36 ends 39
P Flooring [2d] <- N, starts 36 ends 38
Q Final inspection [1d] <- O,P, starts 39 ends 40

This is a single path sequential chain from A through Q except E branches to F/G/H merge to I. The critical path likely includes the longest of F/G/H before I. F and H 3d, G 2d; with early start 17, finish 20. Then I at 20, etc. J at 21-23. 

Disruption: J needs 2 extra workdays -> J duration 4d, finishes at 25 instead of 23. K starts 25, L 26, M 29? Let's recalc from J. J start 21, duration 4 -> end 25. K (1d) start 25 end 26. L (3d) start 26 end 29. M (6d) start 29 end 35. N (3d) start 35 end 38. O (3d) start 38 end 41. P (2d) start 38 end 40. Q (1d) <- O,P start 41 end 42. So project finish delay = 2 workdays. Since J is on critical path (no float), completion date slips by 2 days.

Need output JSON with fields. Decision pass/fail/needs_more_info? This is schedule impact assessment; no dangerous or non-compliant. We can decide "pass" maybe meaning assessment conclusion? In previous tasks decision relates to component pass/fail. Here task asks report delay. Not inspection. Probably decision "pass" (valid schedule?), but disruption causes delay. Could use "pass" because no fail conditions. risk maybe medium due to delay. work_stage in-progress. percent_complete? Overall progress maybe around mid-project, but we can estimate based on workday original 40 and current around workday 23? We can estimate percent complete maybe ~55%. But percent_complete field not critical. component_conditions empty. value=2. sound_source empty, confidence 0.9, event_time 0, rate 0, order empty, workable: since J delayed 2 days, work after J is blocked until J and K done? But task asks what work can still start. With early-start, during J delay maybe K,L etc cannot start. But predecessor constraints: Activities before J are already done. J is in progress delayed. Activities independent earlier could proceed? All earlier complete. Current stage probably at J. So workable list maybe empty? Or activities with all predecessors satisfied and not blocked by delay: O/P/Q etc cannot start. Maybe if resources? According to schedule only J can be worked on; others are successors blocked. So workable = [] maybe. However, if some tasks are not dependent on J? N depends on M which depends L etc. All later path blocked. F/G/H done? I done? J in-progress. So no other activity can start. But if assessment asks identify work that can proceed vs blocked, we can list none. Hmm.

Findings: J duration extension due to material delivery slip; J is on critical path with zero float; delay propagates through K-Q, pushing final inspection and project completion by 2 workdays. Actions: expedite insulation material delivery or add resources/crew overtime to compress J back to 2 days; if not recoverable, update schedule baseline and notify stakeholders of 2-day slip. rationale: Critical path runs through J and all downstream activities have finish-to-start dependencies; 2-day extension consumes zero float, so f…
